focus: HARDENING: stress-test MiniMax routing heuristics against concrete scenarios and finalize pattern-index rows plus cross-CLI routing table

graphEvents.label: MiniMax selected for 180k-token multi-file review when configured

label: Pattern index should add exactly three link-only MiniMax rows: context-budget adoption, direct-provider routing heuristics, and prompt-quality/variant-ablation policy.
MiniMax routes for large evidence bundles and quota isolation, but Qwen remains the better tight cheap edit route and DeepSeek remains the hard-reasoning default when budget fits.
Cognition-Pro exhaustion must fail fast rather than silently substituting MiniMax; MiniMax can be offered only as an explicit separate-pool route.
The cross-CLI quality card needs a compact routing table spanning DeepSeek, Qwen, Kimi, GLM, and MiniMax, plus Budget Awareness updates for glm-5.1 and minimax-2.7.
sk-prompt-models remains a sentinel; MiniMax pattern bodies live in cli-opencode, cli-devin, or sk-prompt canonical files.
MiniMax direct provider omits --variant by default until live ablation proves accepted and beneficial behavior.
Current pattern-index.md has 14 rows and a four-column row format suitable for link-only MiniMax additions.
Current cli_prompt_quality_card.md Budget Awareness omits glm-5.1 and minimax-2.7, so the quality-card routing delta should update that list.

direction: Add MiniMax-specific permissions, fallback, or duplicate output-verification rows to pattern-index.md
reason: Existing rows already cover provider-neutral permissions, registry-driven fallback, and output-shape-driven verification; MiniMax-specific policy belongs in owner docs.
